item.title: Leveraged ETFs Sold $6 Billion of Korean Chips in Tuesday Rout
item.description: Leveraged exchange-traded funds tracking Samsung Electronics Co. or SK Hynix Inc. were likely forced to sell a combined $6 billion of the Korean chipmakers’ shares Tuesday to maintain leverage ratios, underscoring how such products can amplify market moves, according to Bloomberg Intelligence.

item.title: Kioxia’s Executive Pay Jumps After AI Boosts Demand and Stock
item.description: Executive pay at Japanese flash memory maker Kioxia Holdings Corp. surged, a reflection of runaway global demand for AI-related chips.

item.title: PBOC Adviser Sees Potential Rate Cut But Calls for Targeted Aid
item.description: China may still cut interest rates this year, though besides looser policy the economy requires more targeted support for technological innovation and improving people’s livelihoods, according to an adviser to the central bank.

item.title: Qatar Sees LNG Output Returning to Normal Within Weeks, FT Says
item.description: Qatar plans to return to normal liquefied natural gas output within weeks from the undamaged parts of its facility, Prime Minister Sheikh Mohammed bin Abdulrahman Al-Thani said in an interview with the Financial Times.

item.title: Tencent Tests DeepSeek-Powered AI Agent for WeChat Corporate App
item.description: Tencent Holdings Ltd. is preparing to launch an AI agent for its Slack-like enterprise communication app, intensifying a high-stakes battle among Chinese tech giants to lock users into their ecosystems in the post-ChatGPT era.

item.title: ByteDance Seeks $20 Billion in Its Largest-Ever Offshore Loan
item.description: ByteDance Ltd., the developer of TikTok, is in preliminary talks with banks for a borrowing of about $20 billion, people familiar with the matter said, in what would be the firm’s largest offshore loan yet at a time when it’s boosting investments in artificial intelligence.

item.title: BlackRock-Backed AIP, KKR Said to Court Stack Asia Data Centers
item.description: The Artificial Intelligence Infrastructure Partnership and Brookfield Asset Management Ltd. are among possible bidders for Stack Infrastructure Inc.’s data centers in the Asia-Pacific region, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Trump Says Justice Department Looking Into Gasoline Prices
item.description: US President Donald Trump has ordered the Department of Justice to look into gasoline prices, which he says aren’t falling fast enough.

item.title: SoftBank Seeks Stake in Japan’s Top Utility to Power AI Boom
item.description: SoftBank Group Corp. is looking to invest in Japan’s biggest power utility to help secure the electricity needed to expand in artificial intelligence, the company’s chief executive officer said.

item.title: EU Rides Out Budget Spat Without Spooking Markets or Ratings
item.description: The European Union’s regime for monitoring public finances has arguably just passed its toughest health test since a revamp after the pandemic.

item.title: RBI Governor Says It Is Premature to Discuss Rate Hikes Yet
item.description: India’s central bank Governor Sanjay Malhotra pushed back against expectations for future interest-rate increases, saying it was “premature” to discuss the policy outlook given ongoing geopolitical uncertainties.

item.title: India’s Shapoorji Pallonji Group Seeks Fresh Bond Extension as Refinancing Drags
item.description: India’s Shapoorji Pallonji Group has offered bondholders a 30-basis point fee in exchange for approving another extension on debt owed by its unit, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Philippines Bans GoreBox After Three Killed in School Shooting
item.description: The Philippine cybercrime agency has temporarily banned online game GoreBox after initial police investigations showed that one of the two students allegedly involved in this week’s school shooting was an avid player.

item.title: China Unfazed by World’s Ire on Trade as Premier Touts Openness
item.description: Beijing’s No. 2 official played down worries about the disruption wrought by his country’s surging exports on the global economy, championing the benefits provided to the world by China’s development.

item.title: Canada to Send Warships to Indo-Pacific, Defense Minister Says
item.description: Canada plans to send warships to the Indo-Pacific in two deployments this year, its defense minister said, part of a move to deepen military ties with regional allies even as Ottawa also endeavors to build closer trade relations with China.

item.title: Indian Tech’s Nifty Share Shrinks to Record Low on AI Worries
item.description: India’s software exporters are steadily losing their sway on the country’s stock market as concerns over artificial intelligence-led disruption trigger a prolonged selloff in the sector.

item.title: Double, Double Toil and Bubble Trouble
item.description: This probably isn’t when it bursts, but there’s room for plenty of over-leveraged damage.

item.title: New Hedge Funds Are Using AI Bots to Rival Industry Giants
item.description: Advances in artificial intelligence are leveling the field for fund managers, making it easier for boutique firms to compete with big macro and bond investors.

item.title: China’s Independent Oil Refiners Slash Runs to Nine-Year Low
item.description: China’s independent oil refiners have slashed operating rates to a nine-year low, highlighting the lingering impact of the US-Iran war on the leading importer of Tehran’s crude.

item.title: Zhipu Said to Weigh Multibillion-Dollar Share Sale in Hong Kong After 2,000% Gain
item.description: Chinese artificial-intelligence model maker Zhipu is considering a share sale to raise several billion US dollars in Hong Kong, people familiar with the matter said, after soaring 2,000% since its listing in January.

item.title: Philippine Tycoon Boosts PhilWeb Investment in Online Gaming Push
item.description: Philippine billionaire Lance Gokongwei is taking a 15% stake in online gaming technology firm PhilWeb Corp., boosting the company’s foray into artificial intelligence and the tycoon’s exposure to the gaming industry.

item.title: Copper Holds Losses as Dollar and Hawkish Fed Pressure Metals
item.description: Copper and industrial metals largely held Tuesday’s slump as a stronger dollar and the Federal Reserve’s hawkish stance weighed on the demand outlook.

item.title: Mild Singapore Inflation Points to Policy Hold, Economists Say
item.description: Singapore will likely hold monetary policy at its meeting next month and consider further moves later in the year after weaker-than-expected inflation data for May, according to economists.

item.title: Chinese Zinc Traders Eye Export Window to Clear Domestic Glut
item.description: Chinese zinc producers are eagerly waiting for exports to become more profitable to help clear a glut of the metal at home.

item.title: SoftBank’s Masayoshi Son Scraps Plan to Retire in His Sixties
item.description: SoftBank Group Corp.’s Masayoshi Son said he plans to hold onto the top spot at the technology group he founded for another decade or more, shredding his long-held plan to hand over the reins in his sixties.

item.title: Conservative Fujimori Poised to Win Peru’s Presidency, Vote Count Shows
item.description: Conservative Keiko Fujimori is poised to become Peru’s next president after running her fourth consecutive campaign, according to figures published by the elections regulator after weeks of adjudicating disputed ballots.

item.title: India Lets NRIs Leverage Deposits to Draw Dollars
item.description: RBI swap covers only principal, not interest.

item.title: AirPods Maker Luxshare Gauges Interest for $3 Billion Hong Kong Listing
item.description: Electronics manufacturer Luxshare Precision Industry Co. has started gauging interest for a Hong Kong listing that may raise about $3 billion, according to people familiar with the matter who asked not to be identified to discuss a private matter, moving toward what could be one of the city’s biggest deals this year.

item.title: China Weakens Yuan Fixing for Fourth Session as Dollar Advances
item.description: China’s central bank set the daily guidance rate for the yuan weaker for a fourth straight session, showing flexibility in the currency management to the US dollar’s advance.

item.title: Bank of Korea Says Higher Rates Needed Amid Housing, Debt Risks
item.description: South Korea’s central bank reinforced its hawkish shift, saying higher interest rates will be needed at an appropriate time as rising home prices, household debt and leveraged investment threaten to stoke financial imbalances.
